title: Allana Djo Solo
content:

{{Quote|Destiny has but one throne, and if the Jedi queen claims it, the Sith cannot.|Sarasu Taalon, to Luke Skywalker, after seeing Allana seated on the Throne of Balance|<ref name="Vortex">''[[Fate of the Jedi: Vortex]]''</ref>}}
'''Allana Djo Solo''' was the daughter of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]] and Jedi Knight [[Tenel Ka Djo|Tenel Ka Chume'Ta Djo]], [[Queen Mother]] of [[Hapes/Legends|Hapes]]. In addition to her [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitivity]], she was by the blood of her father, the niece of the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]] and [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Anakin Solo]], the granddaughter of the heroes of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebellion]], [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia]] and [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], the grand niece of legendary [[Grand Master/Legends|Jedi Grand Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], and the great-granddaughter of the Jedi Knight turned [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] and the Senator of Naboo [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]]. As the [[Chume'da]] of the [[Hapes Consortium/Legends|Hapes Consortium]], Allana was expected to ascend to the Hapan throne in succession to her mother. She was purported to have [[Death/Legends|died]] in [[41 ABY]] during the [[Battle of Uroro Station]], after the [[Council of Moffs/Legends|Moff Council]] sanctioned the use of a [[Nanovirus|nanokiller]], designed to target the [[Hapan Royal House|royal Hapan bloodline]], of which Allana was a direct matrilineal member.

In reality, posing as a war orphan named '''Amelia''', Allana was placed by Tenel Ka in the custody of her grandparents who officially adopted her as "'''Amelia Solo'''", after her mother received a warning through the Force from Darth Caedus of the Moff's plan. Tenel Ka hoped that the arrangement would keep Allana safe, while allowing the child to [[Jedi training/Legends|learn]] the ways of the Jedi.

Later, several individuals, including Allana's great-uncle, [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], received a [[Force vision/Legends|vision]] similar to one that Jacen Solo had had before, in which an older Allana was a [[Queen/Legends|queen]] seated upon a [[Throne of Balance|throne]]. As the [[Lost Tribe of Sith]] wanted to conquer [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] for themselves, they saw the potentially enthroned "Jedi queen" as a threat to their plans. While Allana's identity as the being in the visions was discovered by the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] and the dark side entity [[Abeloth]], they were defeated by the Jedi, and before she headed to [[Shedu Maad]] to train as a Jedi, the girl made her identity as Chume'da Allana Djo Solo public during the Hapan reception of [[Wedding of Jagged Fel and Jaina Solo|Jagged Fel and Jaina Solo's wedding]].

==Biography==
===Birth, Dark Nest Crisis, and Swarm War (35 ABY–36 ABY)===
{{Quote|You're not married, and you had a baby? You guys are going to be in so much trouble!|Ben Skywalker, to Allana's parents|<ref name="uq" />}}
[[File:Tenelka-nec.jpg|thumb|left|220px|[[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]] and [[Tenel Ka Djo]], Allana's parents]]
When Jacen negotiated with Tenel Ka about borrowing a [[Hapan]] fleet to help the [[Killik/Legends|Killiks]] in their [[Swarm War|battle]] against the [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]], she agreed - on the condition that he spend one night with her; they did spend the night together, resulting in Jacen impregnating Tenel Ka.<ref name="The Joiner King">''[[Dark Nest I: The Joiner King]]''</ref>

Tenel Ka carried and concealed the baby for twelve months by using [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], and one week after Allana was born, an [[Attempted assassination of Allana Solo|assassination attempt]] was made on her life. Due to this assassination attempt, the couple decided to keep the identity of the baby's father a secret, in order to protect her from angered Hapan nobles. [[Ben Skywalker]], who was still [[Joiner|joined]] to the [[Gorog]] nest, accidentally discovered Allana's paternity; all mention of Allana and the visit to [[Hapes/Legends|Hapes]] was [[Memory rub/Legends|wiped]] from his mind by Jacen.<ref name="uq" />

Even during the Jedi convocation at [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]], Jacen and Tenel Ka would not reveal that Jacen was Allana's father to [[Han Solo/Legends|Han]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Solo]], Allana's paternal grandparents. Leia surmised her son knew who the father was, but did not suspect Jacen. [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] speculated that Tenel Ka's reluctance to talk about Allana meant that she was blemished in some way—a fate the Hapans considered to be worse than death.<ref name="The Swarm War">''[[Dark Nest III: The Swarm War]]''</ref>

===Second Galactic Civil War (40 ABY–41 ABY)===
====Assassination attempts====
{{Quote|Jedi are supposed to be strong!|Allana, to [[Darth Caedus|her father]]|<ref name="Tempest" />}}
During the [[Second Galactic Civil War]], Allana and her mother were marked for death—first by Jacen Solo, as a sacrifice for his Sith training,<ref name="Tempest" /> although he later chose his parents for that role instead,<ref name="Exile">''[[Legacy of the Force: Exile]]''</ref> and then by Corellian leaders, as part of a plot to remove Tenel Ka and her heir from the Hapan throne in order to let a pro-Corellian relative take control. Her paternal grandparents, still unaware of their blood ties to Allana, were propelled to act on account of their relationship with Tenel Ka.

[[File:Allana_Solo.jpg|thumb|200px|Young Chume'da Allana playing with [[Jacen (stuffed creature)|Jacen]], a [[Stuffed Tauntaun Toy]]]]
Allana served as the primary motivation for Jacen's extreme actions, a reason owing to his love for her, which was considerably more heartfelt than his love for any other being, including Tenel Ka. After a near-successful assassination attempt on Allana and Tenel Ka by the assassin [[Aurra Sing/Legends|Aurra Sing]]—then under the alias "Nashtah"—Tenel Ka decided to leave Allana with her father on the [[Anakin Solo (starship)|''Anakin Solo'']] until it was safe enough for her to return to Hapes.<ref name="Tempest" />

However, even as a [[Battle of Hapes (Second Galactic Civil War)|dangerous battle]] raged over Hapes, the Star Destroyer's security was not enough to deter Sing, who easily infiltrated the ship and defeated Allana's security droid. Only the personal intervention of her father Jacen saved the young Chume'da, as he kept the assassin from reaching Allana despite many personal injuries. When Sing was momentarily incapacitated, Allana injected her with a personal defense drug, knocking her out. She was later reunited with her mother on Hapes, although she again had to be hidden from the Skywalkers and Solos.<ref name="Tempest" />

During a private moment, Jacen discovered he actually cared more about Allana than Tenel Ka, as he thought of his daughter's welfare on a daily basis. Shortly after becoming co-Chief of State and leaving [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], Jacen visited Allana to determine if Allana and Tenel Ka were meant to be his sacrifice. He gave Allana a stuffed tauntaun, which she named "Jacen," and he left shortly thereafter, only to kill [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Skywalker]] and take the name Darth Caedus.<ref name="Sacrifice">''[[Legacy of the Force: Sacrifice]]''</ref>

====Kidnap and reunion====
{{Dialogue|Allana|You're Jacen's mommy. He's my daddy.|Leia Solo|I know; I'm your grandmother.|Han Solo|Hi, sweetie; I'm your grandfather.|attr=Allana revealing Jacen as her father and Han and Leia Organa Solo introducing themselves to their granddaughter|src=<ref name="Fury" />}}
Later after Tenel Ka turned against Caedus by withdrawing all Hapan military support from the Galactic Alliance, Caedus vowed to kill anyone, including Tenel Ka, if she stood between him and his daughter. Caedus then kidnapped Allana from Tenel Ka in an effort to blackmail her into again pledging Hapes' support to the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|GA]]. Allana was replaced by a young orphan girl, Tika, who strongly resembled Allana in her looks. Caedus told Allana that people were trying to kill her so as to prevent her from complaining about being taken away from her mother. Tenel Ka had told Allana that anyone who took care of her would first have to provide a secret phrase, that together, they made up. Caedus never provided the words, frightening her in the process. Allana woke up from unconsciousness, telling Caedus that she hated him, after she hit him with a pass out gas dart that Tenel Ka had given her; it wasn't strong enough, though.

Caedus then revealed to Allana that he was her father, calming her down, quickly forgiving him, and she embraced him, saying that she loved him.<ref name="Fury">''[[Legacy of the Force: Fury]]''</ref>

Tenel Ka went to Luke Skywalker for help, and Luke agreed to rescue Allana from Caedus, leading a strike team to the ''Anakin Solo'', where he, Ben, and [[Saba Sebatyne]] distracted Caedus, while Han and Leia rescued Allana. While returning Allana to Hapes, Han and Leia finally discovered their blood relationship between themselves and Allana; they were her grandparents.

Caedus, meanwhile, angered that he had lost his daughter, targeted his rage at [[Patra Tebut]], Force-choking her until her neck snapped.<ref name="Fury" />

After the Jedi Order abandoned [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] as their base of operations, moving their headquarters to [[Shedu Maad]], in the [[Transitory Mists]], Leia later commented on how the proximity to Hapes would make visiting Allana much easier and more convenient.<ref name="Revelation">''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]''</ref>

====Staged death (41 ABY)====
{{Quote|That… that was my sacrifice.|Darth Caedus's thoughts|<ref name="Revelation" />}}
During the last stages of the war, Allana was kept close to her mother at all times, as a security precaution. When the nanokiller designed by the [[Council of Moffs/Legends|Moff Council]] targeting all those with Hapan royal blood was released on the ''[[Dragon Queen]]'', Tenel Ka's flagship, most of the crew was killed, including Allana, apparently.<ref name="Invincible" />

However, Allana's "death" was actually a cover story concocted by Tenel Ka, in order to provide a safer environment for her daughter. She had faked Allana's death, sending out a message over the Holonet that Allana was one of the nanokiller's victims.

In reality, she released Allana into the care of her grandparents, [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]], and [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], and Allana began training at the Jedi Academy, under the guise of a war orphan named '''Amelia'''.<ref name="Invincible" /> She was introduced under her alias to her aunt, [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]], after Jaina had killed Allana's father [[Battle of Uroro Station|in a lightsaber duel over Shedu Maad]].

As a tribute to Allana's "death", the Moff Council, under new [[High Moff]] [[Jagged Fel]] founded the [[Imperial Mission]], as suggested by Allana's grandfather, Han Solo.<ref name="Invincible" />

===Travel with the Solos (41 ABY–44 ABY)===
====About the ''Millennium Falcon''{{'s}} past====
[[File:Allana_Solo_Standefer.jpg|thumb|left|138px|Allana during her time as "Amelia" Solo.]]
In [[43 ABY]], the eager, inquisitive, and Force-strong Allana discovered a strange object resembling an antiquated [[transponder]] aboard her grandfather's beloved spacecraft, and went with her grandparents on a journey to uncover the past of the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']]. On Taris, during her trip with Solos, Allana was kidnapped, but saved by the rogue Jedi [[Seff Hellin]]; after this event, she noticed similarities between the way Jacen and Seff felt in the Force.

====A voice on Kessel====
Later in the same year, as Han and Leia journeyed into [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel's]] caverns to save [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian's]] [[Calrissian-Nunb Mines|mining facility]] from destruction, [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] were charged with Allana's welfare; however, she managed to convince Threepio to let her outside onto the planet's surface, and when she moved out of the droids' range, she made her way to a mysterious tomb; there, she used the Force to reach out to it and heard a voice telling her that it could "feel her". The voice asked her who was there, begging her to respond and requesting her name. She later left the tomb and reunited with the droids.<ref name="Outcast">''[[Fate of the Jedi: Outcast]]''</ref>

Allana later accompanied her grandparents on a [[Mission to save Kessel|mission]] to halt the destruction of Kessel, and was present for an encounter with a Kessel [[energy spider]].

====Coruscant Livestock Exchange and Exhibition====
The Solos later attended the [[Coruscant Livestock Exchange and Exhibition]], partially on the basis that Leia believed it would be beneficial for Allana to have a pet. Jedi Knights [[Natua Wan]] and [[Radd Minker]] tagged along for security. Allana wanted a [[Tauntaun/Legends|tauntaun]], but Han denied her, stating the creature's unpleasant smell. Allana decided she wanted to go to the Dangerous Animal level, and her grandparents reluctantly agreed. While viewing animals such as [[Nexu/Legends|Nexu]] and a [[Rancor/Legends|rancor]], Natua Wan succumbed to [[Force psychosis]]. Natua made her way to the control room, where she locked the doors to the exhibit and disabled the forcefields and corrals holding the animals in the complex. As Radd Minker cut a hole through the door, Han held Allana, and together, they managed to escape the madness.

Later that night, when the animals had been captured, and Natua Wan apprehended, Allana told Han and Leia that she had witnessed Leia killing a Nexu with a litter of cubs. Allana suggested that they adopt one of the cubs; otherwise, they would have no being to care for them. Her grandparents were once more reluctant, but they adopted a cub, which they named [[Anji]].

====Growing troubles====
[[File:Allanasolo.jpg|thumb|right|175px|Allana Solo]]
Shortly thereafter, Allana was left in the care of [[Bazel Warv]] while Han and Leia went to meet with [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Cilghal]] about the insane Jedi. This alarmed Cilghal, since one of the few patterns noticed in the insane Jedi was that they had all stayed at [[Shelter]] during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War|Yuuzhan Vong invasion]], like Warv. Cilghal's fears were realized later that day, although Allana had already left his presence.

That night, the Solos and [[Anji]] were watching ''[[The Perre Needmo Newshour]]'' when the broadcast concerned the events occurring earlier that day, specifically the insanity of Warv and [[Yaqeel Saav'etu]]. Leia listened to the stories and the Solos' conversation, and reflected that she and Han should not try to shelter Allana, as she was the future Queen Mother of Hapes. She then had a vision of Allana on a white throne, mirroring the visions experienced by Darth Caedus and Luke Skywalker.

After receiving word of Luke and Ben Skywalker's experiences in the Maw, Leia and Han hurried to [[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomir]] to help them, reluctantly bringing Allana along. They ordered her to stay in the ''Falcon'', watched over by C-3PO, [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], [[Zekk]], and [[Taryn Zel]]. Allana quickly got bored, and after Artoo disappeared, helped rescue him from the devious mechanic [[Monarg]].

Shortly after they got back from Dathomir, Allana caught C-3PO behaving uncharacteristically, and discovered that he was helping Luke translate the Keshiri language spoken by the Sith that Luke was teamed up with. Allana needed significant comforting to assure her that Luke was not going to the dark side - or joining the Sith.<ref name="Fate of the Jedi: Allies">''[[Fate of the Jedi: Allies]]''</ref>

===Jedi Youngling (44 ABY–)===
Following the defeat of the Lost Tribe of Sith and Abeloth, Allana Djo Solo's true identity of Chume'da, first heir to the Hapan Throne, was revealed publicly during the [[Wedding of Jagged Fel and Jaina Solo|marriage of her aunt]], Jaina Solo, while the beginning of her Jedi training on [[Shedu Maad]] was planned.<ref name="Apocalypse" />

Allana was mentioned by Han Solo as part of the memories her grandmother had lost after she [[Golden flash|merged]] with the Force at the monolith.<ref name="Crucible">[[Crucible (novel)|''Crucible'']]</ref>

===Possible future===
[[File:Anjiandme.jpg|thumb|left|350px|Allana and Anji]]
In [[41 ABY]], shortly before his death, Darth Caedus had a vision of his daughter Allana as a tall young woman standing in the middle of a large crowd of mixed species in front of a large white throne, unifying the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]].<ref name="Invincible" /> This contrasted his previous vision at the [[Pool of Knowledge]] during his travels, where he saw the [[Darth Krayt|Dark Man]] on the [[Throne of Balance]]—with Allana standing at his side. This vision was the catalyst for Jacen's eventual transformation into Caedus: Jacen ended his travels immediately after the vision, determined to change what he saw. The vision he had of Allana on the white throne was proof that Caedus succeeded in changing the future, but by doing so he released [[Abeloth]], the Bringer of Chaos.<ref name="Apocalypse" />

[[43 ABY|Two years later]], Leia Organa Solo had a similar vision of the young girl standing in front of a mix of species, looking to her for guidance and peace. In Leia's vision, Allana was also standing beside a large white throne, but no one was standing beside her.<ref name="Abyss">''[[Fate of the Jedi: Abyss]]''</ref>

In 43.5 ABY, Luke Skywalker had a similar vision to those of his sister and nephew, only this time Allana was surrounded by what looked to be her friends, in front of a white throne.<ref name="Abyss" /> When [[Ben Skywalker|Ben]] looked into the Pool of Knowledge, he saw that he was among the friends who surrounded her. However, when the [[Lost Tribe of Sith|Sith]] [[High Lord]] [[Sarasu Taalon]] also looked into the Pool of Knowledge, he was very displeased to see a Jedi queen, whom he did not know was a relative of the Skywalkers, because he believed that if a Jedi queen rose to power, the Sith could not take over [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="Vortex" /> After Abeloth was defeated, Jacen revealed that the Dark Man on the throne with Allana standing at his side was the reason for his turn.<ref name="Apocalypse" />

==Powers and abilities==
As the daughter of [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]] and [[Tenel Ka Djo]], and the great-granddaughter of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], the [[Chosen One/Legends|Chosen One]],<ref name="The Swarm War" /> Allana possessed a very strong connection to the Force and survived deadly encounters. From an early age, her father taught her the [[Force power/Legends|power]] of [[Force stealth]] for her protection and she was able to [[Force healing/Legends|heal]] herself as her [[Han Solo/Legends|grandfather]] noted that she was a fast healer, like her mother. Allana demonstrated the powers of [[Precognition/Legends|Precognition]] and the Jedi [[Mind trick/Legends|Mind trick]], and used [[Force bellow]] to escape her attacker by stunning him. Allana also exercised a natural ability for [[Animal bond|Animal friendship]] like her father in her relationship with her pet [[Nexu/Legends|Nexu]] and received a [[Force vision/Legends|Force vision]] of the [[Barabel/Legends|Barabel]] nest being uncovered by the [[Lost Tribe of Sith]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].<ref name="Apocalypse" />

When upset, she could [[Hatred|emanate]] the dark side against perceived threats. She also employed the power of Force flight.<ref name="Millennium Falcon novel">[[Millennium Falcon (novel)|''Millennium Falcon'']]</ref>

She was a skilled markswoman and her father believed, should she have fallen to the dark side, she would have been such a powerful [[Sith apprentice/Legends|servant]] to Darth Krayt that he would have achieved lasting victory.<ref name="Apocalypse" />

==Behind the scenes==
According to [[Troy Denning]] (a.k.a. TailOne), in his official thread on [[TheForce.net]] forums, Allana was named after the founder of the Force-sensitive [[Witches of Dathomir]], [[Allya/Legends|Allya]], and the brother of Jacen Solo, [[Anakin Solo]] (All + Ana = Allana).<ref name="JCF">{{JCFCite|url=posts/23110149|board=Authors and Artists|thread=The Official Troy Denning Discussion Thread|user=Tailone|date=2006-02-08|time=8:55 PM|archivedate=20160112024235}}</ref> She was not named in ''The Unseen Queen'' because Tenel Ka wanted Jacen to have a part in choosing her name.
